news.headline: NeurAxis Declares Q2 Stock Dividend on Series B Preferred (~80,463 Common Shares)
news.summary: NeurAxis declared its scheduled quarterly paid-in-kind stock dividend on the Series B preferred, issuing roughly 80,463 new common shares (also disclosed via an accompanying Form 8-K). It is a routine, non-cash obligation under the preferred terms, but it adds modestly to the share count and is a small ongoing dilution item to track.

news.headline: NRXS Named Among 22 Biotech Stocks to Watch
news.summary: A TheStreet Pro screen of 22 biotech/medtech names to watch featured NRXS. Inclusion in sector round-ups adds incremental retail visibility for a thinly traded micro-cap, though it is media commentary rather than a company event.

news.headline: NeurAxis Added to Russell 3000E Growth Benchmark
news.summary: In the course of the annual Russell reconstitution, NRXS was added to the Russell 3000E Growth benchmark. This brings passive index buying and more institutional visibility - the stock marked its 52-week high of $9.33 on June 27.

news.headline: Neuraxis (NRXS) Revenue Breakdown: Business Segments, Regional Revenue & Profit Contribution
news.summary: A financial-media breakdown of Neuraxis's revenue by segment and geography, centered on the IB-Stim (PENFS) pediatric neuromodulation franchise. It underscores how concentrated current sales still are in the core abdominal-pain/IBS indication and the early payer-coverage ramp.

news.headline: NeurAxis Stockholders Approve Directors and Equity Compensation Plans
news.summary: At the annual meeting on June 10, six directors were elected, along with a significant increase to the equity incentive plan and a new ESPP. Relevant because of the additional potential dilution from stock-based compensation.

news.headline: NRXS: Record Q1 revenue and expanded coverage drive growth in pediatric and VA neuromodulation markets
news.summary: Analyst update: record Q1 revenue and growing insurance coverage drive the business; alongside pediatrics, the Veterans Affairs market is also cited as a growth area. It confirms the bullish analyst view following the Category I CPT code launch, backed by Q1 revenue of $1.61M (+80% YoY) and ~86% gross margin.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 Earnings + Business Update
catalysts.detail: Second full quarter with the Category I CPT code; the market expects continuation of the ~80% growth, margin trend, and an update on cash burn/going concern.

catalysts.label: Further payer coverage announcements
catalysts.detail: Conversion of additional insurers following the CPT code; base currently ~100M covered lives - each new policy is share-price relevant.

catalysts.label: Commercial ramp of the RED device
catalysts.detail: Rectal Expulsion Device (FDA 510(k) Dec 2024, its own CPT code, ~$1.5B TAM) - the market is waiting for first revenue contributions as a second pillar alongside IB-Stim.

catalysts.label: IB-Stim/PENFS indication expansions (FDA)
catalysts.detail: Pipeline for further indications (functional dyspepsia, adult/VA population); studies ongoing, submission dates not confirmed.

pulse: NRXS remains in an acceleration phase following the Category I CPT code (effective Jan 1, 2026): Q1 revenue +80% YoY, gross margin ~86%, roughly 100M covered lives, and June inclusion in the Russell 3000E Growth benchmark. The stock has roughly quadrupled over a year (52-week high $9.33 on June 27, since consolidating toward the ~$7 area), and analysts (Craig-Hallum, Zacks) stay bullish with targets of $9-13. Early-July news flow was largely housekeeping - a routine Series B stock dividend and an 8-K plus a sector-feature mention - signaling no operational change but a small dilution drip. The key setup now hinges on the mid-August Q2 print confirming the revenue ramp, further payer-policy wins, and the RED device launch; main risks remain the going-concern note, ongoing ATM dilution, and high micro-cap volatility.
